info.title: Check Point Browse Security API
info.description:
Unified API for Check Point Browse Security — policy management and asset visibility.

## Authentication
All endpoints require a valid CloudInfra JWT token passed as a Bearer token in the `Authorization` header.

### Obtaining a JWT Token
1. Go to the [Infinity Portal API Keys page](https://portal.checkpoint.com/dashboard/settings/api-keys).
2. Click **New** → **New Account API Key**.
3. In the **Service** dropdown select **Browser Security** and create the key.
4. Use the generated API key to obtain a JWT token from CloudInfra.
5. Include the JWT in every request:
   ```
   Authorization: Bearer <JWT_TOKEN>
   ```

## Rate Limiting
Default limits vary per endpoint group. Exceeding any limit results in a 5-minute block period.

action.title: Action
action.description: Action to take for access control

download_file_protection.description: File download protection action. Must be 'na' for GenAI rules; must not be 'na' for Browse rules.

upload_file_protection.description: File upload protection action. Must be 'na' for GenAI rules; must not be 'na' for Browse rules.

AccountSelectionMode.title: AccountSelectionMode
AccountSelectionMode.description:
Account selection mode enumeration.

Determines which accounts/organizations the policy applies to:

- ALL: Policy applies to all tool instances (e.g., cloud desktop, ChatGPT desktop, etc.),
  regardless of whether they are connected to an account/organization or not.

- SELECTED_ACCOUNT: Policy applies only to tool instances (e.g., cloud desktop, ChatGPT
  desktop, etc.) that are connected to one of the specified account IDs (organization IDs
  in some tools' terminology). Tool instances not connected to any account or connected
  to accounts not in the list will not be affected by this policy.

order.title: Order
order.description: Position in the rulebase; lower values are evaluated first and take precedence

source.title: Source
source.description: Users or groups this rule applies to; empty means all users

AddAccessRuleRequest.title: AddAccessRuleRequest
AddAccessRuleRequest.description:
Create a new Access rule.

Access rules control which AI services and applications users are allowed
to interact with, including allow/block decisions per service.

AddChatsRuleRequest.title: AddChatsRuleRequest
AddChatsRuleRequest.description:
Create a new Chats rule.

Chats rules control data-loss-prevention for AI chat interactions:
prompt/response inspection, sensitive-data detection, and file transfer policies.

AddMCPServerRuleRequest.title: AddMCPServerRuleRequest
AddMCPServerRuleRequest.description:
Create a new Agents rule.

Agents rules govern agent interactions, including which tools
agents can invoke and what access controls apply to agent operations.

AddSecureBrowsingRuleRequest.title: AddSecureBrowsingRuleRequest
AddSecureBrowsingRuleRequest.description:
Create a new Secure Browsing rule.

Secure Browsing rules define threat-prevention policies for web browsing:
phishing protection, password-reuse detection, and domain-based filtering.

AttachAccessObjectsRequest.title: AttachAccessObjectsRequest
AttachAccessObjectsRequest.description:
Attach reusable objects to an Access rule.

Access rules support FILE_EMULATION objects (file scanning/emulation settings)
and DOMAIN_SERVICES objects (domain allow/block lists).

rule_id.description: UUID of the rule to attach objects to

feature.title: Feature

AttachChatsObjectsRequest.title: AttachChatsObjectsRequest
AttachChatsObjectsRequest.description:
Attach reusable objects to a Chats rule.

Chats rules support DOMAIN_SERVICES objects (domain allow/block lists
applied during chat interactions).

rule_id.title: Rule Id
rule_id.description: UUID of the rule to attach objects to

feature.title: Feature
feature.description: Object feature type; Secure Browsing supports PROTECTED_DOMAINS and EXCLUDE_DOMAINS

AttachSecureBrowsingObjectsRequest.title: AttachSecureBrowsingObjectsRequest
AttachSecureBrowsingObjectsRequest.description:
Attach reusable objects to a Secure Browsing rule.

Secure Browsing rules support PROTECTED_DOMAINS (domains where threat
prevention is enforced) and EXCLUDE_DOMAINS (domains exempted from scanning).

category_id.description:
Category ID from the supported URLF categories. The following categories are available:

| Category | ID |
|---|---|
| Uncategorized | 0 |
| Computers & Internet | 1 |
| Education | 3 |
| Entertainment | 5 |
| Financial Services | 7 |
| Gambling | 9 |
| Government / Military | 11 |
| Greeting Cards | 12 |
| Hacking | 13 |
| Hate / Racism | 14 |
| Health | 15 |
| Illegal / Questionable | 17 |
| Illegal Drugs | 18 |
| Job Search / Careers | 21 |
| Media Streams | 23 |
| News / Media | 24 |
| Newsgroups / Forums | 25 |
| Nudity | 26 |
| Personals / Dating | 30 |
| Phishing | 31 |
| Political / Legal | 32 |
| Pornography | 33 |
| Real Estate | 34 |
| Recreation | 35 |
| Religion | 37 |
| Restaurants / Dining / Food | 39 |
| Sex Education | 41 |
| Shopping | 42 |
| Alcohol / Tobacco | 45 |
| Art / Culture | 47 |
| Blogs / Personal Pages | 49 |
| Business / Economy | 51 |
| Software Downloads | 53 |
| Sports | 54 |
| Spyware / Malicious Sites | 55 |
| Tasteless | 56 |
| Translation | 58 |
| Travel | 59 |
| Vehicles | 60 |
| Violence | 61 |
| Weapons | 62 |
| Botnets | 65 |
| Spam | 66 |
| Inactive Sites | 67 |
| Fashion | 68 |
| Non-Profits / NGOs | 69 |
| Sex | 70 |
| General | 71 |
| Nature / Conservation | 72 |
| Child Abuse | 73 |
| URL Filtering | 74 |
| Lifestyle | 75 |
| Lingerie / Swimsuit / Suggestive | 76 |
| Suspicious Content | 77 |
| Marijuana | 78 |
| Web Advertisements | 50000032 |
| Instant Chat | 50000086 |
| Very Low Risk | 51000001 |
| Low Risk | 51000002 |
| Medium Risk | 51000003 |
| High Risk | 51000004 |
| Critical Risk | 51000005 |
| Anonymizer | 52000038 |
| P2P File Sharing | 52000046 |
| Instant Messaging | 52000047 |
| Media Sharing | 52000051 |
| Games | 52000058 |
| Social Networking | 52000069 |
| Email | 52000130 |
| Search Engines / Portals | 52000132 |
| File Storage / Sharing | 52000136 |
| Keyloggers | 60530540 |
| Occult | 60530541 |
| Hosting Sites | 60530542 |
| DDNS / Dynamic DNS | 60530648 |
| Artificial Intelligence (AI) | 60517663 |
| Generative AI Tools | 60531762 |

**Note:** GenAI rules only allow category 60531762 (Generative AI Tools), used as "Any AI tool". Browse rules can use any *other* category.

DomainMatchMode.title: DomainMatchMode
DomainMatchMode.description:
Domain matching mode enumeration for remote server domain matching.

Matching modes:
- EXACT_HOST: Matches the exact host/domain
  e.g., "sub1.sub2.something.com"
- ANY_SUBDOMAIN: Matches any subdomain of the specified domain
  e.g., "something.com" matches "sub1.something.com", "sub2.something.com", etc.

objects.title: Objects
objects.description: Reusable policy objects attached to this rule, keyed by feature (e.g. FILE_EMULATION, DOMAIN_SERVICES). Values are lists of object IDs. Objects allow sharing configuration (domain lists, file-protection settings) across rules

URLFCategory.description: URLF Category enumeration with display names as keys and category IDs as values.

URLMatchMode.title: URLMatchMode
URLMatchMode.description:
URL matching mode enumeration for remote server URL matching.

Matching modes:
- EXACT_URL: Matches the exact full URL
  e.g., "http://sub1.sub2.something.com/alsohere/something"
- URL_CONTAINS: Matches if the URL contains the specified string
  e.g., "alsohere" matches any URL containing "alsohere"

Update an existing domains object.

All fields are optional; only provided fields are updated.
If object_data is provided, it replaces the existing domain list entirely.
